title: Replace colossus AI moveToward with adapter-based steering
description:
Consolidate the 3 duplicated `moveToward` functions (behavior.js, titan.js, wraith.js) into a single physics-aware steering function using the adapter.

**Current state:** Each colossus has its own `moveToward` that directly mutates `position` — bypassing physics entirely.

**New approach:**
- Create `colossus/steering.js` with `moveToward(body, target, speed, dt, adapter)`
- Use `adapter.setVelocity` or `adapter.applyForce` to move the kinematic body toward the target
- Keep the clamped-step pattern to prevent overshoot: compute desired velocity, let physics handle it
- Delete the local `moveToward` copies from titan.js and wraith.js — use the shared one
- 2D version for ground colossi (sentinel, titan), 3D version for flying colossi (wraith)

**TDD:** Test that steering produces correct velocity vectors, respects speed limits, handles zero-distance gracefully, and works for both 2D/3D modes. Mock the adapter.

**Files:** colossus/steering.js (new), colossus/behavior.js, colossus/titan.js, colossus/wraith.js

title: Wire colossus meshes as kinematic bodies with collision events
description:
Add cannon-es kinematic bodies for each colossus that sync position/rotation from the AI behavior each frame, and emit collision events for combat.

**Per colossus type (sentinel, wraith, titan):**
1. Create kinematic body matching the colossus bounding shape (approximate: capsule for sentinel, elongated box for wraith, sphere for titan shell)
2. Each frame: `adapter.setPosition(colossusBody, aiState.position)` + set rotation
3. Register collision events between player body and colossus bodies

**Combat integration:**
- Player weapon hits: use `adapter.raycast` from player position in attack direction, check if it hits a colossus body or a specific part
- Colossus attacks: collision event fires when colossus body contacts player — apply knockback force via `adapter.applyImpulse`

**Wind/shockwave forces:**
- `colossus/wraith.js getWraithWindForce`: apply as `adapter.applyForce` on player body
- `colossus/titan.js getShockwaveForce`: same

**TDD:** Test colossus body creation, position sync, and collision event registration with mock adapter. Test raycast hit detection for combat.

**Files:** colossus/sentinel.js, colossus/wraith.js, colossus/titan.js, player/combat.js, game/main.js

title: Consolidate audio param factory tests
description:
engine/audio.test.js has ~25 tests for get*Params functions (getFootstepParams, getClimbingGrabParams, getHeartbeatParams, getSwordSlashParams, getColossusImpactParams, getWeakPointHitParams, getColossusDeathParams, getAmbientWindParams) that mostly check 'does this function return an object with these properties' and 'is charged/destroyed bigger than normal'. These are glorified config lookups.

Consolidate each get*Params function to: 1 structural test (has required fields) + 1 behavioral test (charged vs normal comparison). Keep the shouldPlayHeartbeat and shouldPlayFootstep tests as they have real timer logic.

Expected: ~20 tests removed, ~8 tests kept for param functions.
acceptance_criteria: Each get*Params function has at most 2 tests. shouldPlayHeartbeat/shouldPlayFootstep unchanged. node --test passes.

title: Keep cape and particles as cosmetic-only (no physics engine)
description:
Decision issue: cape Verlet chain and world particles should NOT be migrated to cannon-es.

**Rationale:**
- Cape uses Verlet integration with distance constraints — well-suited for cloth, overkill for a physics engine
- Particles are purely cosmetic (visual dust/embers) with no collision
- Moving them to cannon-es would add overhead for no gameplay benefit
- They already work and have full test coverage

**Action items:**
1. Document this decision in a comment at the top of `player/cape.js` and `world/particles.js`
2. Wind forces on the player should still go through the adapter (wind is a force source, not a physics system)
3. Wind forces on particles can remain direct (particles are cosmetic)
4. Verify cape receives player position from the adapter (already does via state)

**No code changes beyond documentation comments.**

title: Merge overly granular factory property tests
description:
Several test files test factory functions with one test per property instead of one test checking all properties:

1. world/particles.test.js: createParticle has 5 separate tests (position, velocity, lifetime, maxLifetime, size) — merge into 1
2. player/combat.test.js: findHitWeakPoint duplicates filtering logic already covered by trySlash — reduce from 5 to 2-3
3. colossus/sentinel.test.js: 'total patch count is reasonable' tests arbitrary bounds (>100 && <2000) — remove
4. game/progression.test.js: 'progress.defeated is a Set' tests implementation detail — remove
5. engine/input.test.js: minor overlap in WD/Arrow normalization and isTouchInButtonRegion center/inside — trim 2

Expected: ~10 tests removed.
acceptance_criteria: createParticle tested in 1 test. findHitWeakPoint reduced to 2-3 tests. Arbitrary bound checks removed. Implementation detail tests removed. node --test passes.

title: Phase 6: Polish - Audio, music, post-processing, ending, mobile, gamepad, UI
description: Procedural audio system (all sounds), layered music system, post-processing (bloom, color grading), win state / ending sequence, mobile touch overlay, gamepad support, UI refinement (stamina arc, title card, credits)

title: Procedural audio: all sound effects via Web Audio API
description: Implement all sound effects using Web Audio API (engine/audio.js). Ambient wind (filtered noise + LFO), footsteps (noise bursts + bandpass), climbing grabs (filtered clicks), stamina low (heartbeat pulse), sword slash (metallic resonance), colossus impact (low-freq boom), weak point hit (resonant ding + reverb), colossus death (evolving drone).

title: Layered music system with crossfading between game states
description: Implement layered ambient music. Base layer (always playing, warm drone), exploration layer (adds when roaming), combat layer (adds rhythmic pulse + tension near colossus), victory layer (ascending harmony after final kill). All layers crossfade smoothly between states.

title: Win state, ending sequence, and credits
description: After defeating all 3 colossi: sky opens up revealing cosmic vista, islands rise and converge, final silent scene with camera and world, wanderer at convergence point, title card fade in, credits roll while player can still move. TDD-test progression state machine (game/state.js, game/progression.js).

title: Mobile touch overlay and gamepad support
description: Extend input system for mobile and gamepad. Mobile: virtual joystick (left), touch drag look (right), jump/grab/attack buttons (bottom right), semi-transparent, auto-detected via touch events. Gamepad: left stick move, right stick look, standard button mappings. TDD-test gamepad input mapping and touch zone calculations.
notes: Implemented mobile touch overlay and gamepad support via TDD (45 new tests). Pure functions: classifyTouchZone, calculateJoystickVector, normalizeStick, mapGamepadButtons, mapGamepadToInput, isTouchInButtonRegion. Classes: TouchInputManager (virtual joystick, touch look, action buttons), GamepadInputManager (stick/button mapping with configurable dead zones and sensitivity). All 62 tests pass.

title: UI: stamina arc, title screen, minimal HUD, and post-processing
description: Implement minimal UI (engine/ui.js): stamina bar as thin arc around player (white to red), colossus health shown as fading opacity (no bar), direction hints (faint light pillars). Title screen: game title on blurred golden sky, instant gameplay start. Post-processing: bloom on weak points, color grading. No pause menu beyond simple resume/quit overlay.

title: Phase 4: World Building - Traversal paths, wind currents, fog, particles, hub island
description: Connect islands with traversal paths, wind current mechanics, fog/atmosphere layers, particle systems (dust, wind), colossus direction indicators, hub island with shrine

title: Hub island with shrine and colossus direction indicators (light pillars)
description: Create the hub/start island (world/island.js) with a shrine/monument. Implement direction indicators: light pillars or wind particle streams pointing toward each colossus. Player starts here, safe zone.

title: Traversal paths: bridges, stepping stones, and wind current gliding
description: Connect islands with traversal paths (world/paths.js). Natural-looking narrow bridges, stepping stones, floating debris. Wind currents the player can ride for gliding segments. TDD-test path connectivity and wind current force vectors.

title: Fog layers, atmospheric particles (dust/pollen), and wind visualization
description: Implement volumetric-ish fog between island layers (world/fog.js). Floating dust/pollen particles. Wind visualized by particles and cape movement. Distant colossi visible as silhouettes. Mostly Three.js particle/fog rendering.

title: Trim constant-validation and trivial accessor tests
description:
Remove tests that only check config values (e.g. TIDE_TITAN_SCALE is 40, CAPE_CONSTANTS has positive NUM_NODES, FALL_CONSTANTS sign checks) and trivial 1-line accessor tests (isStaminaDepleted, isCapeActive, getTitanPhase, isStaminaLow). These pass even with wrong values and test JS object property access rather than game logic.

Files affected:
- colossus/titan.test.js: remove TIDE_TITAN_SCALE is 40 test
- colossus/wraith.test.js: remove WIND_WRAITH_SCALE is 30 test
- colossus/sentinel.test.js: remove STONE_SENTINEL_SCALE is 20 test
- player/stamina.test.js: remove isStaminaDepleted (2 tests) and isStaminaLow (2 tests)
- player/cape.test.js: remove CAPE_CONSTANTS section (5 tests) and isCapeActive (trim to 2)
- player/fall.test.js: remove FALL_CONSTANTS section (5 tests)
- player/character.test.js: delete entire file (5 tests) — only tests constructor defaults

Expected: ~25 tests removed
acceptance_criteria: All removed tests identified in audit are gone. node --test passes. No behavioral test coverage lost.

title: Replace player movement physics with cannon-es adapter
description:
Refactor `player/movement.js` to use the physics adapter instead of manual Euler integration.

**Changes:**
- `applyMovement`: call `adapter.setVelocity(body, {x, y, z})` instead of mutating state.velocity directly
- `applyJump`: call `adapter.applyImpulse(body, {x: 0, y: JUMP_FORCE, z: 0})` instead of setting velocity.y
- `applyGravity`: REMOVE entirely — cannon-es handles gravity natively
- `updatePlayer`: call `adapter.getPosition(body)` to read back position after step
- Ground collision: handled by cannon-es trimesh collider on island mesh, read `isGrounded` from contact events
- Keep all existing function signatures — the adapter is an implementation detail

**TDD:** Existing movement tests must still pass. Add tests verifying the adapter is called correctly (mock the adapter).

**Files:** player/movement.js, player/movement.test.js

title: Replace fall and climbing physics with adapter
description:
Refactor `player/fall.js` and `player/climbing.js` to use the physics adapter.

**fall.js:**
- `enterFall`: use `adapter.setVelocity` to zero Y, set gravity multiplier via adapter
- `updateFall`: REMOVE manual gravity integration — read position from adapter after step. Fall-specific gravity can be a force scale applied per-frame
- `respawn`: `adapter.setPosition` + `adapter.setVelocity(zero)`

**climbing.js:**
- `tryGrab`: `adapter.setPosition` to snap to surface + `adapter.setVelocity(zero)` + set body type to kinematic while climbing
- `applyClimbingMovement`: `adapter.setPosition` for direct control while kinematic
- `tryJumpClimb`: `adapter.setPosition` for snap between surfaces
- `releaseGrab`: set body type back to dynamic, adapter handles falling naturally

**TDD:** Existing fall/climbing tests must pass. Add adapter mock tests.

**Files:** player/fall.js, player/fall.test.js, player/climbing.js, player/climbing.test.js

title: Phase 3: First Colossus - Stone Sentinel geometry, AI, climbing, combat, death, arena
description: Stone Sentinel geometry (procedural quadruped), basic AI behavior (patrol, aggro), climbable surface generation on colossus, weak points, combat (sword stab), shake-off mechanic, death animation, arena island

title: Colossus base: procedural geometry generation for Stone Sentinel (quadruped golem)
description: Create colossus base system and Stone Sentinel procedural geometry (colossus/base.js, colossus/sentinel.js). Quadruped golem-like form, ~20x player height. TDD-test geometry generation utilities for consistent output.

title: Colossus AI: behavior state machine (idle, patrol, aggro, stunned, dying)
description: Implement colossus behavior state machine (colossus/behavior.js). States: Idle, Patrol, Aggro, Stunned, Dying. Stone Sentinel: slow patrol, charges when player is spotted. TDD-test all state transitions, triggers, and timing logic.

title: Climbable surfaces and weak points on colossus body
description: Generate climbable surface patches on colossus body. Place weak points (glowing sigils): 2 on back, 1 on head. Detect when player is on a weak point. TDD-test surface patch placement and weak point proximity detection.

title: Combat: sword attack, stab on weak points, shake-off mechanic
description: Implement combat system (player/combat.js). Sword slash attacks weak points only. Stab is charged attack while climbing. Colossus periodically shakes to throw player off (extra stamina drain to hold on). TDD-test damage calculation, weak point hit detection, shake-off timing.

title: Colossus death animation and arena island for Stone Sentinel
description: Implement death animation for Stone Sentinel (slow collapse/dissolve). Create arena island (world/island.js) with rocky terrain and ruins for cover. Mostly Three.js rendering work.

title: Phase 7: Jam Prep - Optimization, testing, deploy, submit
description: Performance optimization, final testing across browsers, deploy to hosting, submit HTML to jam form

title: Performance optimization: LOD, batching, occlusion, shadow quality
description: Optimize for 60fps desktop / 30fps mobile. LOD system for distant geometry. Batch static geometry into merged buffers. Occlusion culling for islands not in view. Single directional shadow map, low resolution, cascade for close range. Distance fog to hide pop-in. TDD-test LOD selection logic and culling thresholds.

title: Integrate input: gamepad and touch overlay support into main loop
description: Wire the new TouchInputManager and GamepadInputManager from engine/input.js into game/main.js. Auto-detect touch/gamepad, show virtual joystick overlay on mobile, poll gamepad in update loop. Logic tested but NOT wired in.

title: Add touch virtual joystick overlay for mobile
description: Create TouchInputManager on canvas. Add semi-transparent CSS joystick circle (left side) and action buttons (right side). Wire getTouchJoystick to player movement, getTouchLook to camera orbit. Auto-detect via TouchInputManager.supportsTouch. Hide when no touch device.

title: Add gamepad input polling and button mapping
description: Create GamepadInputManager. Poll update each frame. Merge getState into input used by movement/combat. Show 'Gamepad connected' hint on first detection. Button prompts in HUD change to gamepad icons when connected.

title: Cross-browser testing and deployment: single HTML bundle
description: Bundle all JS into single polyshadow.html for production. Test on Chrome, Firefox, Safari desktop and iOS Safari / Chrome mobile. Deploy to hosting. Submit HTML to jam form. Ensure < 3s load on 4G.

title: Integrate colossi: spawn, AI behavior, death sequence, climbing surfaces
description: Wire colossus/sentinel.js, colossus/wraith.js, colossus/titan.js, colossus/base.js, colossus/behavior.js, colossus/surface.js, colossus/death.js into game/main.js. Colossi need 3D meshes, spawn on islands, run AI behavior state machines, allow climbing via surfaces, and play death sequences. All logic modules exist with tests but are NOT connected to the renderer.

title: Integrate player systems: climbing, stamina, combat, cape, falling
description: Wire player/climbing.js, player/stamina.js, player/combat.js, player/cape.js, player/fall.js into game/main.js. Player needs to climb colossi surfaces with stamina drain, attack weak points, cape physics, and fall damage. All logic tested but NOT in render loop.

title: Integrate game systems: state machine, progression, UI/HUD, audio, music, camera
description: Wire game/state.js, game/progression.js, engine/ui.js, engine/audio.js, engine/music.js into game/main.js. Need title screen, game state transitions, colossus defeat tracking, stamina arc HUD, post-processing config, procedural sound effects, layered music with crossfading. All modules tested but NOT connected.

title: Wire game state machine into main loop
description: Create GameState instance. On title: show title screen, wait for input. On playing: run game. On victory: play ending sequence (5s). On credits: roll credits (15s). On paused: show pause overlay. transition() on events. Call update(dt) each frame for timed transitions.

title: Wire progression tracker with colossus defeats
description: Create ProgressionTracker. On colossus death complete, call defeatColossus(id). onAllDefeated triggers victory state. Display defeat count in HUD. Track which colossi are defeated for indicator visibility.

title: Add HUD rendering: stamina arc, colossus health, direction hints
description: Create HUD overlay (CSS or canvas). Use UISystem.getStaminaArc for arc rendering around player. getColossusHealthVisual for colossus opacity. getDirectionHint for arrow toward objective. getHUDState controls visibility. Minimal aesthetic: white, thin, transparent.

title: Add title screen with game title and start prompt
description: Use UISystem title methods. Show 'POLYSHADOW' text centered on blurred golden sky. Fade in subtitle 'Press any key to begin' with pulse. On input, hideTitle (2s fade). Hidden during gameplay. Re-show on credits->title transition.

title: Wire procedural audio into game events
description: Create Web Audio AudioContext. On player movement: getFootstepParams + shouldPlayFootstep. On climbing grab: getClimbingGrabParams. Low stamina heartbeat: shouldPlayHeartbeat + getHeartbeatParams. Combat: getSwordSlashParams, getWeakPointHitParams. Colossus impact: getColossusImpactParams. Death: getColossusDeathParams. Ambient wind: getAmbientWindParams.

title: Wire music system with game state crossfading
description: Init MusicSystem with AudioContext. setState based on game state: idle on title, exploration when roaming, combat near colossus, victory after all defeated. Call update(dt) each frame for crossfade. pause/resume on game pause. setMasterVolume from audio settings.

title: Add post-processing: bloom, vignette, color grading
description: Use UISystem.getPostProcessConfig. Apply bloom (UnrealBloomPass from three/addons) with bloomIntensity/bloomThreshold. Vignette via custom shader or CSS overlay. Color grading shifts: warm in exploration, desaturated in combat. Weak points glow via bloom.

title: Add ending sequence: sky opening, islands converging, credits
description: On all-defeated victory: sky dome color shifts to cosmic (dark blue/purple with stars), fog fades, defeated colossus islands slowly move toward hub, golden light converges. Camera pans up. After 5s, credits text fades in. Player can still move during credits. After 15s, return to title.

title: Integrate world generation: islands, fog, particles, wind into main game loop
description: Wire world/island.js, world/fog.js, world/particles.js, world/wind.js, world/wind_currents.js, world/indicators.js into game/main.js. Currently game/main.js only has a player box and sky. Need to generate floating islands around the player, add volumetric fog, particle systems, wind effects, and colossus direction indicators. These modules exist with tests but are NOT connected to the renderer.

title: Add procedural island terrain meshes to scene
description: Generate and render floating islands using world/island.js. Create a createIslandMesh(island) function in renderer.js that builds a PlaneGeometry from heightData with vertex colors (brown/green/stone). Generate a hub island (createHubIsland) + 3 arena islands (createArenaIsland for sentinel/wraith/titan). Place islands at fixed positions in the world. Player should be able to walk on island surfaces using getIslandSurfaceHeight for ground collision instead of GROUND_Y=0.

title: Add wind system with visual currents and player physics
description: Integrate world/wind.js and world/wind_currents.js. Run wind system update each frame. Create wind currents between islands using createSteppingStonesPath or bridge paths. Apply wind force to particles and player movement (slight push). Visualize currents as faint flowing line particles.

title: Add atmospheric particle system (dust/embers)
description: Integrate world/particles.js. Create a Points-based particle system with ~200 particles. Use updateParticleSystem with wind from wind.js each frame. Particles should be small glowing sprites (ember/dust aesthetic). Recycle dead particles.

title: Add direction indicators toward undetected colossi
description: Integrate world/indicators.js. Create 3 direction indicators (one per colossus). Use updateIndicators each frame with player position and colossus positions. Show faint light pillars in the direction of nearest undetected colossus. Fade out indicators for defeated colossi.

title: Add fog layers as volumetric particle planes
description: Integrate world/fog.js into the scene. Create semi-transparent fog planes at the 3 default layer heights (gold/warm/gray). Use updateFogSystem each frame, adjust plane opacity via calculateFogDensity at camera height. Fog drifts and density animates over time via noise.

title: Add bridge/stepping stone paths between islands
description: Integrate world/paths.js. Create paths connecting hub island to each arena island. Use generatePathPoints for stepping stones. Render as flat stone meshes floating between islands. Player can walk on them (ground collision with path height sampling).

title: Add fog layers as volumetric particle planes
description: Integrate world/fog.js. Create semi-transparent fog planes at 3 default layer heights. Use updateFogSystem each frame, adjust plane opacity via calculateFogDensity at camera height.

title: Add atmospheric particle system (dust/embers)
description: Integrate world/particles.js. Create Points-based particle system with ~200 particles. Use updateParticleSystem with wind each frame.

title: Add direction indicators toward undetected colossi
description: Integrate world/indicators.js. Create 3 direction indicators. Show faint light pillars toward nearest undetected colossus. Fade defeated ones.

title: Integrate colossi: spawn, AI behavior, death sequence, climbing surfaces
description: Wire colossus/sentinel.js, colossus/wraith.js, colossus/titan.js, colossus/base.js, colossus/behavior.js, colossus/surface.js, colossus/death.js into game/main.js. Colossi need 3D meshes, spawn on islands, run AI behavior state machines, allow climbing via surfaces, and play death sequences. All logic modules exist with tests but are NOT connected to the renderer.

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Stone Sentinel
description: Build Three.js meshes from sentinel definition (colossus/sentinel.js createSentinelDefinition). Use createColossusBody to get parts. Create box/cylinder geometries for each body part (torso, hips, head, 4 legs, 2 back runes, 2 arm runes). Scale by STONE_SENTINEL_SCALE=20. Apply stone material (dark gray with slight vertex color variation). Position parts relative to parent hierarchy. Animate idle breathing (subtle scale pulse).

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Tide Titan
description: Build Three.js meshes from titan definition (colossus/titan.js createTitanDefinition). Large shell (dome), underbelly, head, 2 claws, 4 legs, 2 runes. Scale by TIDE_TITAN_SCALE=40. Shell has rocky texture, underbelly smoother. Animate shell tilt (getShellTilt), leg movement, claw snapping.

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Wind Wraith
description: Build Three.js meshes from wraith definition (colossus/wraith.js createWraithDefinition). Serpentine body: chain of spheres/segments for neck, chest, tail sections. Wings as flat triangulated planes. Head with horns. Scale by WIND_WRAITH_SCALE=30. Pale/translucent material. Animate wing flap and serpentine undulation.

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Stone Sentinel
description: Build Three.js meshes from sentinel definition (colossus/sentinel.js). Box/cylinder geometries for 12 body parts. Scale by 20. Stone material. Animate idle breathing.

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Tide Titan
description: Build Three.js meshes from titan definition (colossus/titan.js). Shell dome, underbelly, head, claws, legs, runes. Scale by 40. Animate shell tilt, legs, claws.

title: Wire colossus AI behavior state machines into update loop
description: Run updateBehavior (sentinel), updateWraithBehavior, updateTitanBehavior each frame. Pass player position, colossus position, dt. Implement state transitions (idle->patrol->aggro->stunned). Move colossus meshes based on AI position output. Trigger shake-off when shouldShakeOff. Handle shouldAttack for damage to player.

title: Implement climbing on colossus surfaces
description: Use colossus/surface.js patches + player/climbing.js. When player is near colossus and presses action, findNearestClimbableSurface. Attach player to colossus body part. Move player with applyClimbingMovement along surface. Drain stamina via player/stamina.js. Show climbing patches as glowing areas on colossus mesh. Player mesh follows colossus movement while attached.

title: Implement death sequence with dissolve animation
description: When all weak points destroyed, trigger triggerDeath. Run updateDeathAnimation each frame. Apply getPartTransform to each body part mesh (kneel, collapse, dissolve). Fade opacity, offset positions, shake camera via getDeathShakeIntensity. When isDeathComplete, call progression.defeatColossus and remove colossus from scene.

title: Add weak point glow visuals and hit feedback
description: Show weak points as glowing runes on colossus body (use buildCombatWeakPoints positions). Render as small emissive sphere meshes. On hit (trySlash/tryStab), flash white then fade. Destroyed weak points turn dark. Bloom post-processing highlights active weak points.

title: Integrate player systems: climbing, stamina, combat, cape, falling
description: Wire player/climbing.js, player/stamina.js, player/combat.js, player/cape.js, player/fall.js into game/main.js. Player needs to climb colossi surfaces with stamina drain, attack weak points, cape physics, and fall damage. All logic tested but NOT in render loop.

title: Replace box player with procedural character mesh
description: Replace the current box mesh with a humanoid figure: head (sphere), body (box), arms (thin boxes), legs (thin boxes), cape (line segments from player/cape.js). Apply updateChain each frame for cape Verlet simulation. Material: dark cloth with vertex color variation.

title: Integrate stamina system with climbing and sprinting
description: Add stamina state (createStaminaState) to player. Run updateStamina each frame with isClimbing/isSprinting flags. Drain on sprint (SPRINT_DRAIN_RATE=15) and climb (CLIMB_DRAIN_RATE=12). Regen when grounded and not climbing. Pass stamina to UI for arc display.

title: Integrate combat: slash, stab, weak point damage
description: Add combat state (createCombatState). On attack input, call trySlash (melee) or startStabCharge/updateStabCharge/tryStab (charged). Use findHitWeakPoint against colossus weak points. Apply damage via applyDamageToWeakPoint. Cooldowns via updateCombat. Trigger audio (getSwordSlashParams, getWeakPointHitParams).

title: Add fall damage and respawn system
description: Use player/fall.js. checkFall each frame for stamina-depleted-while-climbing and threshold breaches. On fall, enterFall with increased gravity. findNearestRespawnPoint on islands. respawn teleports player back. getFreefallCameraData for dramatic camera zoom.

title: Add colossus arena loading and transitions
description: When player approaches colossus direction indicator, transition to colossus arena. Position player at arena spawn point. Spawn colossus at its arena center. After defeating colossus, transition back to hub island. Use createArenaIsland for each colossus type.

title: Remove structural property-enumeration tests in colossus files
description:
titan.test.js, wraith.test.js, and sentinel.test.js have ~30 tests that enumerate properties of returned objects without testing logic: 'each patch has the correct format' (12 individual typeof checks repeated across 3 files), 'createBehaviorState returns default state' (checking 14-17 fields of an object literal spread), 'buildCombatWeakPoints each has id/position/health...', 'has the expected part ids' (listing every part from the definition just created).

Replace repetitive patch format validation with a shared validatePatchFormat() helper called once per test file. Trim default state tests to check 2-3 key fields. Remove 'has expected part ids' tests since they just re-read the data that was just constructed. Keep tests that verify logic: normals are unit vectors, health multipliers are correct, climbable/non-climbable classification, geometry type per part.

Expected: ~20 tests removed, 1 shared helper added.
acceptance_criteria: No test file has inline 12-line typeof checks for patch format. Default state tests check max 3 key fields. No 'has expected part ids' enumeration tests. node --test passes.

title: Create pluggable physics adapter abstraction layer
description:
Design and implement `engine/physics-adapter.js` — a thin abstraction over the physics engine (cannon-es) that allows swapping implementations.

**Interface:**
- `createPhysicsWorld()` — returns world instance
- `createBody(opts)` — create rigid body (dynamic, kinematic, static)
- `createTrimeshCollider(geometry)` — static mesh collider for islands
- `addBody(body)` / `removeBody(body)`
- `step(dt)` — tick the simulation
- `applyForce(body, force)` / `applyImpulse(body, impulse)`
- `setVelocity(body, vec)` / `getVelocity(body)`
- `setPosition(body, vec)` / `getPosition(body)`
- `raycast(from, to)` — returns hit or null
- `onCollision(bodyA, bodyB, callback)` — collision events
- `body.userData` — arbitrary data on bodies

**Implementation:** `engine/cannon-adapter.js` using cannon-es loaded via CDN (`esm.sh/cannon-es`).

**TDD:** Test the adapter interface with a mock physics backend. The adapter itself is a thin wrapper — logic stays in game modules, not in the adapter.

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT import cannon-es directly in any game logic file. All physics access goes through the adapter.

title: Phase 5: Remaining Colossi - Wind Wraith and Tide Titan
description: Wind Wraith: flying behavior, aerial combat, swoop-grab mechanic. Tide Titan: multi-phase, water hazards, tilting shell. Unique arenas for each.

title: Wind Wraith: serpentine flying colossus with swoop-grab and aerial combat
description: Implement Wind Wraith (colossus/wraith.js). Serpentine/dragon-like flying creature, ~30x player length. Flies in patterns, swoops to attack. Player must jump onto it from elevated platforms. Wind pushes player. 2 weak points on wings, 1 on neck. Open sky arena with floating platforms. TDD-test flight AI state machine, swoop timing, and wind push forces.

title: Tide Titan: turtle-crab hybrid with multi-phase fight and water hazards
description: Implement Tide Titan (colossus/titan.js). Massive turtle-crab hybrid, ~40x player width. Slow but devastating, shockwaves, submerges arena parts. Unstable climbing surface with tilting shell. 3 weak points on shell, 1 on head (only exposed when stunned). Multi-phase fight. Cloud-covered arena with rising/falling pools. TDD-test multi-phase state machine, tilt physics, and submersion timing.

title: Remove JS purity/idempotency and redundant coverage tests
description:
Several files have tests that verify JavaScript language semantics rather than game logic:

1. 'is pure function' tests (calling f(x) === f(x)) in island.test.js and indicators.test.js — ~4 tests
2. 'does not mutate the original state' tests that just verify spread operator works — fall.test.js, audio.test.js — ~4 tests  
3. Redundant filtering: 'fails on destroyed/inactive' tested in BOTH findHitWeakPoint AND trySlash/tryStab in combat.test.js — ~4 tests
4. Duplicate coverage: 'returns true at exact edge' + 'returns true at center' in isTouchInButtonRegion, 'returns false when exactly at threshold' + 'returns true when below threshold' overlaps in fall.test.js — ~6 tests

Keep immutability tests only where the function is non-trivial (fog.test.js createFogSystem, indicators.test.js updateIndicator). Keep one filtering test per pattern, not duplicated across functions.
acceptance_criteria: No 'is pure function' tests remain. Redundant filtering tests consolidated to one per pattern. Immutability tests only on non-trivial functions. node --test passes.

title: Phase 2: Climbing System - Grab, surface movement, stamina, cape physics, fall/respawn
description: Grab system (detect climbable surfaces), surface-relative movement, stamina system, cape physics (simple spring chain), climb on test geometry, fall + respawn

title: Stamina system with drain/regen/fall mechanics
description: Implement stamina system (player/ module). Climbing and sprinting drain stamina. Stamina regenerates when standing on ground or resting grip. Running out of stamina while climbing causes fall. Visual indicator: vignette pulse + cape stops flowing. TDD-test all stamina logic: drain rates, regen rates, thresholds, fall trigger.

title: Grab and climbable surface detection
description: Implement grab system: detect climbable surfaces near player, attach player to surface, snap to surface normal. Surface-relative movement while grabbed. Jump-climb between grab points. TDD-test surface detection logic, grab point validation, and snap-to-normal calculations.

title: Cape physics with simple spring chain simulation
description: Implement cape physics using a simple spring chain simulation. Cape should flow naturally with wind and movement, and stop flowing when stamina is low. TDD-test spring physics math (constraint solving, force accumulation).

title: Fall detection, freefall camera, and respawn system
description: Implement fall detection when player steps off edge or runs out of stamina while climbing. Freefall with camera drama. Respawn at nearest island edge. No game over screen. TDD-test fall detection thresholds and respawn point selection.

title: Phase 1: Foundation - Three.js setup, camera, input, player, islands, movement, sky
description: Three.js setup with importmap/es-module-shims, camera system (3rd person orbit), input abstraction (keyboard+mouse first), basic player character (box with legs placeholder), procedural island generation (flat rock + grass), movement: walk, run, jump, basic sky and lighting

title: Input: Abstraction layer for keyboard+mouse
description: Create input abstraction layer (engine/input.js) that normalizes keyboard+mouse input into a unified Input object: { move: {x,y}, look: {x,y}, action, attack, jump, sprint, start }. TDD-test the input mapping and normalization logic.

title: Player: Placeholder character with walk/run/jump movement
description: Create player character module (player/character.js, player/movement.js). Start with a simple box-with-legs placeholder. Implement walk, run (sprint with future stamina drain hook), and jump. Movement relative to camera direction. TDD-test movement math.

title: Camera: Third-person orbit camera system
description: Implement a third-person orbit camera that follows the player. Support smooth orbit with mouse look, auto-adjust distance based on context, collision avoidance to prevent clipping through geometry. Pure math/logic for orbit calculations should be TDD-tested where possible.

title: Sky: Basic skybox, lighting, and golden-hour atmosphere
description: Implement basic sky (world/sky.js) with warm gradient (deep gold to soft peach to pale blue), directional sun light at low angle, ambient warm fill. This is primarily Three.js rendering work, no TDD required.

title: Player: Placeholder character with walk/run/jump movement
description: Create player character module (player/character.js, player/movement.js). Start with a simple box-with-legs placeholder. Implement walk, run (sprint with future stamina drain hook), and jump (ground jump). Movement should work relative to camera direction. TDD-test movement math (direction vectors, speed, jump trajectory).

title: World: Procedural island generation (flat rock + grass)
description: Implement procedural island generation (world/island.js) using noise functions. Create flat rock islands with golden grass patches. TDD-test noise and procedural generation utilities (utils/math.js, utils/noise.js, utils/procedural.js).

title: Setup: Three.js importmap, es-module-shims, and index.html scaffolding
description: Set up the zero-build Three.js project structure. Create index.html with es-module-shims importmap pointing Three.js to CDN. Create initial engine/renderer.js skeleton. Ensure bare imports work in the browser without any build step.

title: Setup: Three.js importmap, es-module-shims, and index.html scaffolding
description: Set up the zero-build Three.js project structure. Create index.html with es-module-shims importmap pointing Three.js to CDN. Create initial engine/renderer.js skeleton. Ensure bare imports work in the browser without any build step.

title: Camera: Third-person orbit camera system
description: Implement a third-person orbit camera that follows the player. Support smooth orbit with mouse look, auto-adjust distance based on context, collision avoidance to prevent clipping through geometry. TDD-test orbit math where possible.

title: Input: Abstraction layer for keyboard+mouse
description: Create input abstraction layer (engine/input.js) that normalizes keyboard+mouse input into a unified Input object: { move: {x,y}, look: {x,y}, action, attack, jump, sprint, start }. TDD-test the input mapping and normalization logic.

title: Wire island meshes as cannon-es static trimesh colliders
description:
When island meshes are created in `game/main.js`, also create static trimesh colliders in the physics world from the island geometry.

**Steps:**
1. After `createIslandMesh`, extract the indexed BufferGeometry vertex/index data
2. Call `adapter.createTrimeshCollider(vertices, indices)` with the correct scale/position
3. Add all 4 island colliders (hub + 3 arena) to the physics world at startup
4. Remove the manual `getGroundHeight` function — cannon-es handles ground contact via collision events

**The player body should now collide with island surfaces naturally, with `isGrounded` derived from contact normals.**

**TDD:** Test that trimesh collider data is correctly extracted from island geometry (vertex/index format). Test that ground height via physics raycast matches getIslandSurfaceHeight.

**Files:** game/main.js, engine/renderer.js
